name: Corn Relish
recipeIngredient: 1 Cup onion, small chopped or sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Fresh corn: Remove husks and silks. Cook ears of corn in boiling water for 5 minutes, then remove and plunge into cold water. Drain, and cut corn from the cob without scraping it.
2. Frozen corn: Defrost overnight in the refrigerator or for 2 to 3 hours at room temperature. Place containers in front of a fan to hasten defrosting.
3. Combine peppers, celery, onions, sugar, vinegar, salt, and celery seed in a pan. Cover until mixture starts to boil, then boil uncovered for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Mix dry mustard and turmeric with a small amount of liquid from the boiling mixture; add to the corn along with the boiling mixture. Return to boiling and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. For thickening: Add 1/4 cup flour blended with 1/2 cup water when adding the corn to the cooking mixture. This will help achieve a thicker consistency.
6. Pack the relish loosely into hot pint jars while still boiling, leaving at least 1/2-inch of headspace from the top. Adjust jar lids.
7. Process filled jars in boiling water for 15 minutes (begin counting processing time once the water returns to a boil). Remove jars and ensure complete seals are achieved. Set jars upright on a wire rack or folded towel, spaced several inches apart.
Yields 7 pints.
